english: exam; test; to verify; to check

etymology.description: 耂 ('an old man with long hair, bent over') + 丂 ('a walking cane' originally drawn as 匕) -> an old man leaning on a cane
etymology.interpretation: The character 考(kǎo, 'exam; test; to verify; to check') depicts an old man with long hair and bent over, combined with a cane. This image represents the wisdom and scrutiny often associated with elders, which conceptually extends to the idea of examining, verifying, or testing knowledge or truth.

parts.english: old man with long hair, bent over

examples.chinese: 我下周有一个重要的考试(我下週有一個重要的考試)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ xià zhōu yǒu yī gè zhòngyào de kǎoshì.
examples.english: I have an important exam next week.

examples.chinese: 老师要检查我们的作业(老師要檢查我們的作業)。
examples.pinyin: Lǎoshī yào jiǎnchá wǒmen de zuòyè.
examples.english: The teacher wants to check our homework.

examples.chinese: 为了准备考试(為了準備考試)，他每天都学习到很晚(他每天都學習到很晚)。
examples.pinyin: Wèile zhǔnbèi kǎoshì, tā měitiān dōu xuéxí dào hěn wǎn.
examples.english: In order to prepare for the test, he studies until late every day.
